item.title: Postgres 并发：可能出错的地方（第 2 部分） | Postgres Concurrency : What could go wrong (part 2)
item.description:
在你与 Bob 的最后一次不幸事件之后，你学会了在交易中小心隔离。 时光飞逝，你已经负责过几个小报告。 现在您要为 The Coffee Company 准备一份更具批判性和战略性的报告。

尽管如此，超自然的事情正在公司发生。 您的一位同事告诉您，他发现了鬼魂，需要帮助来追逐它们。

数据库公司里的幽灵

你和他讨论过这个问题，他告诉你，他提供的咖啡单价超过 15 美元，因此他被收取了费用。

但是当他生成他的报告时，有一个相同的咖啡条件导致两个不同的结果集。 他问了别人，这个同事告诉他说数据库里可能有Ghost在读写数据，他有点害怕。

由于您现在对数据库和隔离级别更加熟悉，因此您向他提供了帮助，以便您可以解决这个问题。

首先，您要求他向您展示他必须创建的报告的查询。

 BEGIN SELECT * FROM coffees WHERE unit_price < 15.0; ...// Another query without updating SELECT * FROM coffees WHERE unit_price < 15.0; COMMIT; BEGIN coffee_id | name | unit_price | country -----------+---------------------+------------+------------ 1 | Black Honey | 14.4 | Costa Rica 3 | Las Fincas Granadas | 13.3 | Costa Rica (2 rows) ... coffee_id | name | unit_price | country -----------+---------------------+------------+------------ 1 | Black Honey | 14.4 | Costa Rica 3 | Las Fincas Granadas | 13.3 | Costa Rica 2 | Ambela Hakala | 14.4 | Ethiopia (3 rows) COMMIT

您了解您和您的同事正在经历孤立问题。 但这一次，它很诡异：这是Ghost Read问题。

由于您已经对此进行了试验，因此您决定深入研究 Postgres 文档以了解如何解决此并发问题。

您终于发现在 Postgres 中， Read Repeatable级别可以防止Ghost Read和Unrepeatable Read.

要解决您同事的问题，您必须将隔离级别设置为READ REPEATABLE ，就像您使用UNREPEATABLE READ.

 START TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ SELECT * FROM coffees WHERE unit_price < 15.0; ...// Another query without updating SELECT * FROM coffees WHERE unit_price > 15.0; COMMIT; BEGIN coffee_id | name | unit_price | country -----------+---------------------+------------+------------ 1 | Black Honey | 14.4 | Costa Rica 3 | Las Fincas Granadas | 13.3 | Costa Rica (2 rows) ... coffee_id | name | unit_price | country -----------+---------------------+------------+------------ 1 | Black Honey | 14.4 | Costa Rica 3 | Las Fincas Granadas | 13.3 | Costa Rica (3 rows) COMMIT

没那么可怕

如您所见，我们可能会在 RDBMS 中遇到与并发有关的超自然问题。 这并不可怕，但我们必须小心数据库中的隔离，因为在某些情况下保护和隔离级别可能很低。

事实上， READ REPEATABLE使您能够对抗 Postgres 中的GHOST READ ，但要小心，在另一个 RDBMS 中不可能是这种情况。

隔离、并发和一致性是具有挑战性的问题，您通常必须对它们进行试验才能很好地理解它们。 你可能会面临另一个隔离问题，因为另一个同事更新的一些结果很奇怪，他可能需要你的帮助。



原文:

After your last misadventure with Bob, you learn to be careful with Isolation in your transaction. Times flies, and you have been in charge of several small reports. And now you are in order of a much more critical and strategic report for The Coffee Company.

Nonetheless, paranormal things are happening in the company. One of your colleagues told you that he found ghosts and needs help chasing them.

Ghost in the database company

You discussed this with him, and he told you he had been charged for providing the coffee above a unit price of 15 dollars.

But when he generated his reports, there was a coffee the same where the condition caused two different results sets. He asked this someone else, but this colleague told him that there was maybe some Ghost reading or writing data in the database, so he was a bit scared.

Since you are more familiar with the database now and the isolation level, you offered him help so you can figure this out.

First, you asked him to show you the query for the report he has to create.

BEGIN
SELECT * FROM coffees WHERE unit_price < 15.0; ...// Another query without updating
SELECT * FROM coffees WHERE unit_price < 15.0; COMMIT; BEGIN coffee_id | name | unit_price | country -----------+---------------------+------------+------------ 1 | Black Honey | 14.4 | Costa Rica 3 | Las Fincas Granadas | 13.3 | Costa Rica
(2 rows)
... coffee_id | name | unit_price | country -----------+---------------------+------------+------------ 1 | Black Honey | 14.4 | Costa Rica 3 | Las Fincas Granadas | 13.3 | Costa Rica 2 | Ambela Hakala | 14.4 | Ethiopia
(3 rows) COMMIT

You understand that you and your colleague are experiencing an isolation issue. But this time, it is spooky: this is the Ghost Read issue.

The Ghost Read issue is a concurrency issue where the same where clause does not produce the same results set in a duplicate transaction.

Postgres solution

Since you have experimented with that, you decided to dive deep into the Postgres documentation to see how this concurrency issue can be solved.

You have finally discovered that in Postgres, the Read Repeatable level protects against both Ghost Read and Unrepeatable Read.

To fix the issue of your colleague, you must put the isolation level to READ REPEATABLE like your precedent issue with UNREPEATABLE READ.

START TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ
SELECT * FROM coffees WHERE unit_price < 15.0; ...// Another query without updating
SELECT * FROM coffees WHERE unit_price > 15.0; COMMIT; BEGIN coffee_id | name | unit_price | country -----------+---------------------+------------+------------ 1 | Black Honey | 14.4 | Costa Rica 3 | Las Fincas Granadas | 13.3 | Costa Rica
(2 rows)
... coffee_id | name | unit_price | country -----------+---------------------+------------+------------ 1 | Black Honey | 14.4 | Costa Rica 3 | Las Fincas Granadas | 13.3 | Costa Rica
(3 rows) COMMIT

Not so frightening

As you saw, we can encounter paranormal issues with concurrency in RDBMS. This is not so spooky, but we must be careful about isolation in our database because protection and isolation level can be low in several cases.

Postgres's isolation strategy is not necessarily the same in another RDBMS. Indeed READ REPEATABLE enables you to fight against GHOST READ in Postgres but be careful it could not be the case in another one RDBMS.

Isolation, Concurrency, and consistency are challenging issues, and you often must experiment with them to understand them well. You will likely face another problem with isolation because of some results with another colleagues update are weird and he might need your help.
item.author: yet_anotherdev

item.title: 使用 S3 网关端点降低数据传输成本 | Reducing Data Transfer Costs with S3 Gateway Endpoint
item.description:
当今世界，数据为王。 所有行业的企业都依赖数据的存储、检索和分析来做出明智的决策并保持竞争力。 然而，随着大数据的爆发，云存储已经成为企业存储和分析海量数据的必备工具。 但是，在 VPC 中使用 S3 会产生大量数据传输成本。 要从 VPC 内访问 S3，流量必须通过 Internet 路由并返回，这会增加高昂的数据传输成本，尤其是对于拥有大量数据的企业而言。

幸运的是，Amazon 有解决这个问题的方法——S3 Gateway Endpoint。 在这篇博文中，我们将探讨什么是 S3 网关端点、它的工作原理以及它如何帮助降低数据传输成本。

什么是 S3 网关端点？

它通过 AWS 网络在您的 VPC 和 S3 之间提供安全的私有连接，绕过公共互联网。 这允许您直接从您的 VPC 访问您的 S3 存储桶和对象，就好像它们托管在您的 VPC 中一样。

当您创建 S3 网关终端节点时，AWS 会在您的 VPC 中创建一个映射到 S3 服务的终端节点。 这在您的 VPC 和 S3 之间提供了安全和私有的连接，确保您的数据免受未经授权的访问。

要使用 S3 网关端点，您只需更新路由表以将发往 S3 的流量定向到端点。 这可确保从 VPC 内到 S3 的任何流量都通过 AWS 网络发送到 S3 网关端点，然后再发送到 S3 服务。

请注意，VPC 和 S3 存储桶需要位于同一 AWS 区域中。

如需视觉演示和详细的设置说明，我建议观看此视频教程。

广泛使用 S3 来存储和分析数据可能会产生大量的数据传输成本。 通过 Internet 路由流量并返回访问 S3 存储桶可能会增加高昂的数据传输成本。 但是，实施 S3 网关端点可以显着降低数据传输成本。

通过实施 S3 网关端点，可以直接从 VPC 内访问 S3 存储桶和对象，而不会产生任何数据传输成本。 这使我们能够更有效地存储和访问数据，从而降低总体数据传输成本。

结论

总之，Amazon 的 S3 Gateway Endpoint 是解决从 VPC 内部访问 S3 时的数据传输成本问题的绝佳解决方案。 它在您的 VPC 和 S3 之间提供安全和私有的连接，确保您的数据免受未经授权的访问，同时还允许您直接从 VPC 内访问您的 S3 存储桶和对象，而不会产生任何数据传输成本。



原文:

In today's world, data is king. Businesses across all industries rely on the storage, retrieval, and analysis of data to make informed decisions and remain competitive. However, with the explosion of big data, cloud storage has become a necessary tool for businesses to store and analyze large volumes of data. Amazon S3 is a popular cloud storage service used by millions of businesses worldwide due to its scalability, high durability, and reliability.

However, using S3 from within a VPC can incur significant data transfer costs. To access S3 from within a VPC, traffic must be routed over the internet and back, which can add up to high data transfer costs, especially for businesses with large volumes of data.

Fortunately, Amazon has a solution to this problem - the S3 Gateway Endpoint. In this blog post, we will explore what an S3 Gateway Endpoint is, how it works, and how it has helped reduce data transfer costs.

What is an S3 Gateway Endpoint?

An S3 Gateway Endpoint is a service that allows you to access S3 from within a VPC without incurring any data transfer costs. It provides a secure and private connection between your VPC and S3 over the AWS network, bypassing the public internet. This allows you to access your S3 buckets and objects directly from your VPC as if they were hosted within your VPC.

How Does An S3 Gateway Endpoint Work?

When you create an S3 Gateway Endpoint, AWS creates an endpoint in your VPC that maps to the S3 service. Traffic between the S3 Gateway Endpoint and S3 is routed through the AWS network, avoiding the public internet. This provides a secure and private connection between your VPC and S3, ensuring that your data is protected from unauthorized access.

To use the S3 Gateway Endpoint, you simply need to update your routing tables to direct traffic destined for S3 to the endpoint. This ensures that any traffic to S3 from within your VPC is sent over the AWS network to the S3 Gateway Endpoint and then on to the S3 service.

Note that VPC and S3 buckets need to be in the same AWS region.

For a visual demonstration and detailed setup instructions, I recommend watching this video tutorial.

How S3 Gateway Endpoint Helped Reduce Data Transfer Costs

Using S3 extensively to store and analyze data can incur significant data transfer costs. Routing traffic over the internet and back to access S3 buckets can add up to high data transfer costs. However, implementing an S3 Gateway Endpoint can significantly reduce data transfer costs.

By implementing an S3 Gateway Endpoint, it was possible to access S3 buckets and objects directly from within a VPC, without incurring any data transfer costs. This allowed us to store and access data more efficiently, reducing overall data transfer costs.

Conclusion

In conclusion, Amazon's S3 Gateway Endpoint is an excellent solution to the problem of data transfer costs when accessing S3 from within a VPC. It provides a secure and private connection between your VPC and S3, ensuring that your data is protected from unauthorized access, while also allowing you to access your S3 buckets and objects directly from within your VPC, without incurring any data transfer costs.
item.author: damola12345

item.title: 加强您的三层应用程序：加强应用程序安全性的主动措施 | Fortifying Your Three-Tier Application: Proactive Measures for Strengthening Your Application Security
item.description:
这些层包括表示层、应用程序层和数据库层，每个层在应用程序堆栈中都具有特定的作用。 虽然这种架构提供了许多好处，但它也带来了必须解决的安全挑战，以确保数据和系统的保护。

在本文中，我将探讨可用于强化三层应用程序体系结构并改善部署安全状况的主动措施。 我们将讨论加强应用程序堆栈每一层的技术，以及部署和维护安全的三层应用程序环境的最佳实践。

第 1 层：表示层

它通常由 Web 服务器、负载平衡器和其他处理用户交互的组件组成。 为了保护表示层，可以采取以下措施：

  1. 安全通信协议：使用 SSL/TLS 加密客户端和服务器之间的所有通信。 这将保护敏感数据免遭窃听，并确保传输中数据的完整性。
  2. 访问控制机制：使用防火墙、网络分段和 DMZ 等访问控制机制来控制对表示层的访问。 这将有助于防止未经授权的访问并减少攻击面。
  3. 实施 Web 应用程序防火墙 (WAF)：使用 WAF 来防止常见的 Web 应用程序攻击，例如 SQL 注入、跨站点脚本和跨站点请求伪造。 这将有助于防止基于 Web 的攻击并降低受损风险。
  4. 强化 Web 服务器配置：通过禁用不必要的服务、关闭未使用的端口和删除不必要的软件，确保 Web 服务器配置得到强化。 这将减少攻击面并防止已知漏洞被利用。

此 Terraform 代码片段可用于部署AWS 弹性负载均衡器 (ELB) ：

 resource "aws_elb" "presentation-tier-elb" { name = "presentation-tier-elb" availability_zones = ["${var.aws_region}a", "${var.aws_region}b", "${var.aws_region}c"] listener { lb_port = 80 lb_protocol = "http" instance_port = 80 instance_protocol = "http" } health_check { target = "HTTP:80/" } tags = { Environment = "${var.environment}" } }

第二层：应用层

应用层处理用户请求并管理应用程序逻辑。 它通常由应用程序服务器、中间件组件和处理业务逻辑的其他组件组成。 可以采取以下措施来保护应用层：

  1. 强大的身份验证机制：使用强大的身份验证机制，例如双因素身份验证和密码策略来防止未经授权的访问。 这将确保只有授权人员才能访问应用层。
  2. 安全编码实践：使用安全编码实践来最大限度地降低应用程序代码中存在漏洞的风险。 这包括输入验证、输出编码和错误处理。 这将降低应用程序级攻击的风险。
  3. 数据库访问控制：使用基于角色的访问控制和权限等访问控制来控制对数据库的访问。 这将防止未经授权的访问并降低数据泄露的风险。
  4. 应用程序级加密：使用应用程序级加密来保护敏感数据，例如密码、信用卡信息和个人信息。 这将保护数据免遭未经授权的访问并确保其机密性。

此 Terraform 代码片段可用于部署AWS Elastic Beanstalk环境：

 resource "aws_elastic_beanstalk_environment" "application-tier-env" { name = "application-tier-env" application = "${var.application_name}" solution_stack_name = "${var.solution_stack_name}" setting { namespace = "aws:autoscaling:launchconfiguration" name = "InstanceType" value = "${var.instance_type}" } tags = { Environment = "${var.environment}" } }

第 3 层：数据库层

数据库层负责存储和管理数据。 它通常由数据库服务器、数据存储设备和处理数据管理的其他组件组成。 为了加强数据库层，可以采取以下措施：

  1. 实施数据库级加密：使用数据库级加密来保护静态数据。 这将保护数据免遭未经授权的访问并确保其机密性。
  2. 实施数据库访问控制：使用基于角色的访问控制和权限等访问控制来控制对数据库的访问。 这将防止未经授权的访问并降低数据泄露的风险。
  3. 实施数据库备份和恢复：使用数据库备份和恢复机制确保在发生灾难或数据丢失时可以恢复数据。 这将确保业务连续性并最大限度地减少停机时间。
  4. 强化数据库服务器配置：通过禁用不必要的服务、关闭未使用的端口和删除不必要的软件，确保数据库服务器配置得到强化。 这将减少攻击面并防止已知漏洞被利用。

该模板可用于自动部署和配置数据库层组件。 它是用于部署AWS RDS 数据库的Terraform 代码片段：

 resource "aws_db_instance" "database-tier-db" { identifier = "database-tier-db" allocated_storage = "${var.allocated_storage}" storage_type = "${var.storage_type}" engine = "${var.engine}" engine_version = "${var.engine_version}" instance_class = "${var.instance_class}" username = "${var.username}" password = "${var.password}" db_subnet_group_name = "${aws_db_subnet_group.subnet_group.name}" parameter_group_name = "${var.parameter_group_name}" backup_retention_period = "${var.backup_retention_period}" multi_az = "${var.multi_az}" publicly_accessible = "${var.publicly_accessible}" vpc_security_group_ids = ["${aws_security_group.database-tier-sg.id}"] tags = { Environment = "${var.environment}" } }

结论
要保护三层应用程序，需要一种主动的方法来强化应用程序堆栈的每一层。 每一层都提出了独特的安全挑战，必须解决这些挑战才能确保保护数据和系统。 通过实施访问控制、使用加密、强化服务器配置以及实施备份和恢复机制等最佳实践，您可以降低受损风险并确保保护其关键资产。

使用 Terraform 模板确保一致的配置并降低可能导致安全漏洞的错误配置或人为错误的风险。

通过采取这些主动措施，您可以改善安全状况，降低数据泄露和系统受损的风险，并确保其业务运营的连续性。 安全的三层应用程序环境对于维护用户信任、保护知识产权和遵守法规要求至关重要。 随着对应用程序安全的威胁不断演变，您必须在保护三层应用程序环境的方法中保持警惕和主动。



原文:


The Three-Tier Application architecture is a popular model that allows for the efficient management of application resources by dividing them into three distinct layers. These layers include the presentation, application, and database tiers, each with a specific role in the application stack. While this architecture offers many benefits, it also presents security challenges that must be addressed to ensure the protection of data and systems.

In this article, I will explore proactive measures that can be taken to fortify the Three-Tier Application architecture and improve the security posture of your deployment. We will discuss techniques for hardening each layer of the application stack, along with best practices for deploying and maintaining a secure Three-Tier Application environment.

Layer 1: Presentation Tier

The Presentation Tier is responsible for presenting data to the user and receiving user inputs. It typically consists of web servers, load balancers, and other components that handle user interactions. To protect the Presentation Tier, the following measures can be taken:

  1. Secure Communication Protocols: Use SSL/TLS to encrypt all communication between the client and server. This will protect sensitive data from eavesdropping and ensure the integrity of data in transit.
  2. Access Control Mechanisms: Use access control mechanisms such as firewalls, network segmentation, and DMZs to control access to the Presentation Tier. This will help prevent unauthorized access and reduce the attack surface.
  3. Implementing Web Application Firewalls (WAFs): Use WAFs to protect against common web application attacks such as SQL injection, cross-site scripting, and cross-site request forgery. This will help prevent web-based attacks and reduce the risk of compromise.
  4. Hardening Web Server Configuration: Ensure that web server configurations are hardened by disabling unnecessary services, closing unused ports, and removing unnecessary software. This will reduce the attack surface and prevent known vulnerabilities from being exploited.

Terraform templates can be used to automate the deployment and configuration of the Presentation Tier components. This Terraform code snippet can be used to deploy an AWS Elastic Load Balancer (ELB):

resource "aws_elb" "presentation-tier-elb" {
  name               = "presentation-tier-elb"
  availability_zones = ["${var.aws_region}a", "${var.aws_region}b", "${var.aws_region}c"]

  listener {
    lb_port           = 80
    lb_protocol       = "http"
    instance_port     = 80
    instance_protocol = "http"
  }

  health_check {
    target = "HTTP:80/"
  }

  tags = {
    Environment = "${var.environment}"
  }
}

Layer 2: Application Tier

The Application Tier processes user requests and manages application logic. It typically consists of application servers, middleware components, and other components that handle business logic. The following measures can be taken to secure The Application Tier:

  1. Strong Authentication Mechanisms: Use strong authentication mechanisms such as two-factor authentication and password policies to protect against unauthorized access. This will ensure that only authorized personnel can access the Application Tier.
  2. Secure Coding Practices: Use secure coding practices to minimize the risk of vulnerabilities in the application code. This includes input validation, output encoding, and error handling. This will reduce the risk of application-level attacks.
  3. Database Access Controls: Use access controls such as role-based access control and permissions to control access to the database. This will prevent unauthorized access and reduce the risk of data breaches.
  4. Application-Level Encryption: Use application-level encryption to protect sensitive data such as passwords, credit card information, and personal information. This will protect data from unauthorized access and ensure its confidentiality.

This Terraform code snippet can be used to deploy an AWS Elastic Beanstalk environment:

resource "aws_elastic_beanstalk_environment" "application-tier-env" {
  name                = "application-tier-env"
  application        = "${var.application_name}"
  solution_stack_name = "${var.solution_stack_name}"

  setting {
    namespace = "aws:autoscaling:launchconfiguration"
    name      = "InstanceType"
    value     = "${var.instance_type}"
  }

  tags = {
    Environment = "${var.environment}"
  }
}

Layer 3: Database Tier

The Database Tier is responsible for storing and managing data. It typically consists of database servers, data storage devices, and other components that handle data management. To fortify the Database Tier, the following measures can be taken:

  1. Implementing Database-Level Encryption: Use database-level encryption to protect data at rest. This will protect data from unauthorized access and ensure its confidentiality.
  2. Implementing Database Access Controls: Use access controls such as role-based access control and permissions to control access to the database. This will prevent unauthorized access and reduce the risk of data breaches.
  3. Implementing Database Backup and Recovery: Use database backup and recovery mechanisms to ensure data can be restored in the event of a disaster or data loss. This will ensure business continuity and minimize downtime.
  4. Hardening Database Server Configuration: Ensure that database server configurations are hardened by disabling unnecessary services, closing unused ports, and removing unnecessary software. This will reduce the attack surface and prevent known vulnerabilities from being exploited.

This template can be used to automate the deployment and configuration of the Database Tier components. It is a Terraform code snippet used to deploy an AWS RDS database:

resource "aws_db_instance" "database-tier-db" {
  identifier                     = "database-tier-db"
  allocated_storage              = "${var.allocated_storage}"
  storage_type                   = "${var.storage_type}"
  engine                         = "${var.engine}"
  engine_version                 = "${var.engine_version}"
  instance_class                 = "${var.instance_class}"
  username                       = "${var.username}"
  password                       = "${var.password}"
  db_subnet_group_name           = "${aws_db_subnet_group.subnet_group.name}"
  parameter_group_name           = "${var.parameter_group_name}"
  backup_retention_period        = "${var.backup_retention_period}"
  multi_az                       = "${var.multi_az}"
  publicly_accessible            = "${var.publicly_accessible}"
  vpc_security_group_ids         = ["${aws_security_group.database-tier-sg.id}"]

  tags = {
    Environment = "${var.environment}"
  }
}

Conclusion
To secure a Three-Tier Application requires a proactive approach to fortify each layer of the application stack. Each layer presents unique security challenges that must be addressed to ensure the protection of data and systems. By implementing best practices such as implementing access controls, using encryption, hardening server configurations, and implementing backup and recovery mechanisms, you can reduce the risk of compromise and ensure the protection of their critical assets.

Terraform provides an efficient way to automate the deployment and configuration of the Three-Tier Application environment, making it easier to maintain a secure environment over time. Use Terraform templates to ensure consistent configurations and reduce the risk of misconfigurations or human error that can lead to security vulnerabilities.

By taking these proactive measures, you can improve you security posture, reduce the risk of data breaches and system compromise, and ensure the continuity of their business operations. A secure Three-Tier Application environment is essential for maintaining user trust, protecting intellectual property, and complying with regulatory requirements. As threats to application security continue to evolve, it is imperative for you to remain vigilant and proactive in their approach to securing their Three-Tier Application environment.
